Business Interpretation: Higher (riskier) LendingClub sub-grade increases predicted default risk.
60-month loans carry materially higher default risk than 36-month loans.
Higher bureau credit score reduces predicted default risk.
Higher debt-to-income ratio increases predicted default risk.
Higher market-assigned interest rate signals higher priced-in risk.
Renting (vs owning/mortgage) is associated with higher default risk.
A loan payment consuming more of monthly income increases default risk.
Higher annual income reduces predicted default risk.
A smaller loan relative to income reduces default risk.
Longer credit history reduces predicted default risk.
Higher revolving credit utilization increases default risk.
Shorter employment tenure is associated with higher default risk.
Income verification status has a modest, mixed effect on default risk.
Larger loan amounts are associated with modestly higher default risk.
Loan purpose has a modest effect, varying by stated reason for borrowing.
Any public derogatory record increases default risk.
Higher combined DTI/utilization stress increases default risk.
A recent delinquency increases default risk.
Utilization above 75% has a small additional risk-increasing effect.
